title: Agent-Adoption Specification v1.0 — Scanner Output
description: Canonical JSON output schema for scanners conforming to the Agent-Adoption Specification v1.0. See https://respectarium.com/spec/agent-adoption/v1 for the prose specification (SPEC.md). This schema validates the wire format of a single scan response in normal (non-debug) mode.

specVersion.description: The Agent-Adoption Specification version this scanner conforms to. Semver.

profile.description: Identifier of the profile applied to this scan. References a profile bundled with the specification or a custom profile published by the implementation.

domain.description: The brand root domain (eTLD+1) that was scanned. Lowercase, no scheme, no trailing slash.

finalUrl.description: The fully-qualified URL the scanner ended on after following redirects from the input domain.

scannedAt.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which the scan completed.

score.description: Aggregate score 0-100, computed per the spec's scoring formula (SPEC.md §7). When all scored checks return neutral, the score is 0.

level.description: Assigned level (SPEC.md §8). Integer 1, 2, or 3. v1.0 conforming scanners MUST NOT emit level values outside this set.

levelName.description: Human-readable level label, corresponding 1:1 to the level field. 1=Basic Web Presence, 2=AI-Aware, 3=Agent-Optimized.

categoryReports.description: Per-category reports in the canonical order: discoverability, accessControl, contentReadability, agentEndpoints. Conforming scanners MUST emit exactly these 4 reports in this order.

name.description: Human-readable name of the scanner implementation.

version.description: Version string for the scanner implementation. Format chosen by the implementation; semver recommended.

nextLevel.description: Pointer to the next level the scanned domain has not yet attained, with the unmet check requirements. Conforming scanners MUST emit this field at level 1 and level 2, and MUST OMIT this field entirely when level === 3 (the highest level defined in v1.0).

level.description: The level the domain would advance to upon meeting requirements.

levelName.description: Human-readable label of the next level.

requirements.description: Check IDs that must flip to status 'pass' for the domain to advance. v1.0 emits a single-check gate per level transition.

description.description: Short description of what the category measures.

score.description: Per-category subscore 0-100. Computed using the same formula as the top-level score, scoped to scored checks within this category.

passed.description: Count of all checks (scored + informational) in this category with status 'pass'.

failed.description: Count of all checks (scored + informational) in this category with status 'fail'.

neutral.description: Count of all checks (scored + informational) in this category with status 'neutral'.

checks.description: Per-check results within this category, in scanner-defined order.

id.description: The canonical check ID. Lowercase kebab-case. v1.0 defines 25 IDs (see SPEC.md §5).

category.description: The category this check belongs to. Must match the enclosing categoryReport.category.

status.description: Per-check outcome (SPEC.md §6). pass/fail are binary verdicts; neutral means informational-only or hedge — excluded from scoring math.

scored.description: True if this check contributes to scoring; false if informational. Equivalent to (weight > 0) — both are emitted on the wire for client convenience.

weight.description: Numeric weight per the v1.0 weight table (SPEC.md §5). 0 = informational. Weight 0 implies scored=false; weight > 0 implies scored=true.

message.description: One-line human-readable verdict explaining the status.

description.description: Longer (one-paragraph) description of what the check evaluates and why it matters.

durationMs.description: Wall-clock time the scanner spent executing this check, in milliseconds.

specUrls.description: URLs of relevant external standards, RFCs, or canonical references for the check's subject.

dependsOn.description: Check IDs whose results this check depends on. Empty array if independent.

details.description: Check-specific structured payload. Schema is check-defined; consumers MUST parse defensively. The spec does not constrain the internal shape (additionalProperties: true) — implementations MAY emit any fields here. Conforming scanners MUST NOT include a 'body' key (raw HTTP response bodies belong outside the wire response).

checksEvaluated.description: Number of checks the scanner evaluated. v1.0 defines 25 checks; this field MAY differ from the spec-defined check count in later spec minor versions as checks are added.

checksSkipped.description: Number of checks the scanner did not evaluate (e.g., due to profile filtering or runtime conditions).

scanDurationMs.description: Total wall-clock duration of the scan on the server side, in milliseconds.

counters.description: Implementation-defined counters for telemetry/diagnostics. Each top-level key is a counter group whose value is an object of named integer metrics. Spec does not enumerate counter groups or metric names — additionalProperties is open at both levels.

clusters.description: Cluster verdicts in canonical v1.0 order: htmlPath, spaRenderingCap, noViablePathCap. Top-level normative field — clusters are part of the score-computation discipline (see SPEC.md §7.3) and are required of every conforming scanner.

triggered.description: True when the rendering-strategy check failed and the cluster zeroed page-size-html's weight.

coefficient.description: Multiplier applied to weights of the checks listed in appliesTo. 1 when not triggered (no effect); 0 when triggered (weight removed).

appliesTo.description: Check IDs whose weights are scaled by this cluster's coefficient.

message.description: Human-readable explanation of the cluster's verdict.

triggered.description: True when rendering-strategy fails AND the SPA-shell or invisible-content threshold is crossed.

capScore.description: Score cap applied. 39 = severe (ratio >= 2.0 OR SPA shell short-circuit); 59 = moderate (1.2 < ratio < 2.0). MUST be present when triggered === true; MUST be absent when triggered === false.

triggered.description: True when ALL four agent-content viability checks (llms-txt-exists, rendering-strategy, markdown-url-support, markdown-negotiation) are non-pass AND at least 2 are explicit fail.

capScore.description: Score cap applied (always 39). MUST be present when triggered === true; MUST be absent when triggered === false.
